news.title: India's flexible market powers through global challenges
news.para: Amidst global economic turbulence and geopolitical tensions, India's flexible workspace sector is demonstrating remarkable resilience.  Escalating uncertainties are paradoxically accelerating the shift towards agile solutions, with flex spaces becoming a strategic imperative.  This segment is carving its own growth trajectory, capturing significant market share and positioning itself as a vital enabler for businesses.

news.title: PMAY scam: Lokayukta raids expose ₹29 crore fake billing in Belagavi
news.para: Karnataka Lokayukta raided officials of the Slum Development Board and a private contractor in Belagavi for alleged large-scale irregularities and fake billing in the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban). Investigations revealed collusion to generate fraudulent bills for non-existent houses, leading to the alleged misappropriation of Rs 23.3 crore.

news.title: Pune: Experts fear property prices may rise ₹250-300 per sq ft if RMC suppliers go ahead with proposed hike
news.para: Ready-mix concrete suppliers in Pune have proposed a rate hike, potentially increasing construction costs by Rs 250-500 per sqft. This could lead to higher property prices for new launches as developers face challenges absorbing rising input costs. While some developers may absorb initial increases, future projects may see price corrections to maintain viability in the price-sensitive market.

news.title: Gurugram: 850 shanties razed as DTCP enhances demolition drive in Saraswati Kunj
news.para: Gurugram authorities continued their demolition drive in Saraswati Kunj, Sector 53. Over 850 illegal shanties were razed across 4.2 acres. This action targets reclaiming public land and curbing illegal construction. Residents are calling for uniform enforcement across the city. The area has a history of ownership disputes and unauthorized construction.

news.title: Private developers to build IT infra at Thiruvananthapuram's Technocity
news.para: Technopark is inviting infrastructure developers to build premium IT and ITeS spaces at its Technocity expansion. Offering government-owned land on long-term lease, the initiative aims to attract private investment and meet the rising demand for scalable workspaces from technology companies. This move is expected to accelerate Phase IV's growth and bolster Thiruvananthapuram's standing as a tech hub.

news.title: Chandigarh administration's freehold push to unlock properties worth crores
news.para: Chandigarh administration has approved converting leasehold industrial and commercial plots to freehold ownership. This move aims to unlock properties worth hundreds of crores and boost revenue. The proposal has been sent to the Ministry of Home Affairs for final approval. This is a significant step towards improving ease of doing business in the city.

news.title: Cement companies diverge on expansion plans amid West Asia uncertainty
news.para: Indian cement companies are taking different paths on spending. Some, like Ultratech Cement, Dalmia Bharat, and Nuvoco Vistas, are increasing investments for expansion. Others, including Ambuja Cement and Shree Cement, are slowing down their capital expenditure. This divergence reflects varied growth strategies in the Indian market. The focus remains on India's long-term development drivers.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh minister orders action against errant builders in Noida
news.para: Uttar Pradesh Industrial Development Minister Nand Gopal Gupta Nandi has directed officials to take stern action against builders who have defaulted on payments and to expedite the resolution of disputes with buyers. The minister also flagged alleged irregularities in project allotments and civic contracts. He emphasized the need to address stalled housing projects and pending flat registrations urgently.

news.title: Hyderabad: EOW files case on Saket Engineers for villa mortgage fraud
news.para: Real estate firm Saket Engineers faces a new case in Hyderabad for allegedly mortgaging occupied properties as unsold inventory to secure a ₹30 crore loan. A villa owner discovered his property, already mortgaged to HDFC, was also pledged to another entity. The company claims an error in documentation, stating the loan is repaid and mortgages released.

news.title: WSB invests ₹75 crore in Omaxe Group's projects; Karyan Group to invest ₹900 crore on Ghaziabad project
news.para: WSB Partners has injected ₹75 crore into Omaxe Group's residential plotted projects in Ujjain and Indore for growth capital. Meanwhile, Karyan Group announced a ₹900 crore investment for its Ghaziabad residential project, Trevana Residences, anticipating ₹1,500 crore in revenue from 608 units.

news.title: Jaipur: Delayed action on unsafe building leaves Ramganj residents frustrated
news.para: Residents of Jagannath Shah Ka Rasta are protesting administrative delays over a damaged five-storey building. Evacuated eight days ago due to safety concerns, the building's fate—repair or demolition—remains undecided, leaving nearby families facing daily struggles and long detours for basic access.

news.title: Green nod delay holds up Thiruvanthapuram airport expansion
news.para: Expansion of Thiruvananthapuram International Airport's Terminal 2 is delayed. Environmental clearance is pending with state authorities. The process involves multiple scrutiny stages. This delay impacts the planned April 2025 construction start. The airport operator needs this expansion for future passenger growth. Redevelopment includes terminal modernization, parking, and new facilities.

news.title: Rajkot civic body collects ₹152 crore advance taxes in 40 days
news.para: Rajkot Municipal Corporation has collected Rs 153 crore in advance property and water taxes for the 2026-27 financial year within 40 days. Digital payments led the collection, with significant discounts offered to early payers. This advance collection shows a steady increase from the previous year across all city zones.

news.title: Orissa high court stays suspension on sand quarry lessee
news.para: The Orissa High Court has ruled that statutory authorities cannot usurp powers not expressly conferred by law. The court stayed an interim suspension order on a sand quarry lessee, finding no provision in the Odisha Minor Mineral Concession Rules, 2016, for such immediate action pending adjudication of alleged violations.

news.title: Rajasthan: RIICO gets nearly 800 applications for 465 plots
news.para: RIICO received nearly 800 applications for 465 industrial plots valued at Rs 550 crore, primarily for the Kankani Industrial Area in Jodhpur. To boost occupancy in underutilised zones, RIICO revised its policy, offering plots at 60% of the prevailing rate. This initiative saw a significant rise in applications for these areas.

news.title: NeoLiv acquires 76 acres land in Maharashtra's Khopoli for plotted development, villa project
news.para: NeoLiv has acquired a 76-acre land parcel in Khopoli near Mumbai. This marks their third plotted development in the Panvel-Khopoli belt. The company plans a mixed-use community with villas, plots, and retail. The project has an estimated Gross Development Value of Rs 800 crore. This acquisition is a significant milestone for NeoLiv's expansion in a sought-after micro-market.

news.title: Mohali development body officials submit records to ED in ₹150 crore CLU fraud probe
news.para: Two senior Greater Mohali Area Development Authority (Gmada) officials appeared before the Enforcement Directorate (ED) in Jalandhar regarding a Rs 150 crore money laundering probe. The ED is investigating the approval processes for major real estate projects, examining potential bypasses of regulatory norms. This follows raids on several entities involved in these projects.
